word: G23 - ἀγανακτέω - aganaktéō - ag-an-ak-teh'-o - Be much displeased

sections.paragraph:
- Original: ἀγανακτέω

- Transliteration: Aganakteo

- Phonetic: ag-an-ak-teh'-o

- Definition:

1. to be indignant, moved with indignation, be very displeased

- Origin: from agan (much) and achthos (grief, akin to the base of G43)

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech: Verb

- Strong's: From ἄγαν agan (much) and ἄχθος achhos̄ (grief; akin to the base of G43); to be greatly afflicted that is (figuratively) indignant: - be much (sore) displeased have (be moved with with) indignation.
